classes.reasoning: The document is a peer-reviewed narrative review article published in the journal Vasa (2024, volume 53, issue 6, pages 366-370). It includes formal journal metadata, structured abstract, keywords, references, submission/acceptance dates, ORCID identifiers, and a correspondence address—all hallmarks of a published medical research article.

summary: This is a narrative review article titled "Peripheral arterial disease in women," published in the journal Vasa in 2024. The review examines sex-based differences in the prevalence, risk factors, pathophysiology (especially the role of estrogen), and treatment outcomes of peripheral arterial disease (PAD), with a focus on underdiagnosis, delayed treatment, and the controversial role of hormone replacement therapy in women.
references_to: ACC/AHA 2005 Practice Guidelines for the management of patients with peripheral arterial disease
Rotterdam study (Westendorp et al., Arch Intern Med 2000;160:2498-502)
Women's Health Initiative randomized controlled trial (Rossouw et al., JAMA 2002;288:321-33)
National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey 1999-2004
TASC II Working Group, J Vasc Surg 2007;45 Suppl S:S5-67
